breaking.text: Broadcom reports fiscal Q1 earnings after the bell — analysts expect $2.03 EPS on $19.18B revenue
Coinbase surges 14.6% after Trump signals support for digital asset market bill
Trump backs crypto firms against banks on stablecoin yields, Coinbase surges 14%
Broadcom, Veeva Systems, Okta report after-hours earnings — Broadcom analysts expect $2.03 EPS on $19.18B revenue

summary: The S&P 500 rose 0.78% to 6,869.50 on Wednesday as markets staged a broad recovery from recent Middle East war-driven selling, buoyed by easing oil prices and stronger-than-expected economic data. ADP private payrolls came in at 63,000 vs. 50,000 expected, while the ISM Services PMI held at 53.5 with a cooling prices-paid component, and the Fed Beige Book showed slight-to-moderate growth in seven of twelve districts. Technology and crypto-linked stocks led gains, with Micron and AMD each jumping 5%, while Coinbase surged 14.6% after President Trump signaled support for digital asset legislation. The Nasdaq gained 1.29% to 22,807.48 as the broader tape reflected cautious optimism, though the S&P 500 continues to face technical resistance at the 7,000 level.
